element_introduction_order.element: Avatar character creation and basic movement
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns mouse-driven interface and real-time movement in controlled environment

element_introduction_order.element: Party recruitment and companion AI control
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to manage up to 8 party members with configurable AI behaviors (aggressive, defensive, ranged, melee)
Player learns combat is unpaused; must manage party positioning and tactics without turn-based safety
Player learns keyword-based dialogue options unlock contextually; options only appear after plot-driven discovery
Player learns drag-and-drop mechanics; realizes almost all non-anchored items can be moved, taken, or used
Player discovers items can be stacked to form stairs and access previously blocked areas
Player learns to bake bread, forge weapons, play instruments—world responds to creative item use
Player navigates stacked objects and multi-level environments; movement affected by object placement

element_introduction_order.element: Large-scale Britannia exploration and plot discovery
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player uncovers major plot threats; must cleanse world of monster infestations and schemes

typical_relief_triggers: Pausing to open inventory or character menus
Engaging in dialogue with NPCs (game pauses)
Successfully recruiting allies to reduce solo vulnerability
typical_tension_triggers: AI party members occasionally making self-defeating moves (e.g., friendly fire situations)
Large-scale monster infestations in unexplored areas
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Real-time exploration and combat create continuous tension when traveling; tension releases during safe NPC interaction and inventory management pauses. Early game encourages party building to ease tension; late game escalates with high-stakes plot threats.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Non-anchored items scattered throughout world can be collected and manipulated; optional exploration areas contain craftable items and interactive scenery
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Multi-layered maps hide areas accessible only by stacking items as improvised stairs; creative object placement reveals secret paths

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like focus on interface mastery (mouse controls, dialogue trees, inventory). Monster encounters are manageable; party recruitment reduces combat risk
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Environmental puzzle complexity increases; stacking mechanics demand creative thinking. Monster infestations appear; combat AI becomes critical for success
difficulty_curve.late_game: Large-scale plot threats; extensive world cleansing required. Moral virtue choices carry narrative weight and affect available options
notable_spikes: First real-time combat encounter (shift from turn-based safety)
Introduction of puzzle-solving via item stacking
Major plot revelations requiring multiple party members with specialized roles

notable_level_patterns.description: Safe zones where dialogue trees unlock plot information and keywords are learned. Player gathers party members and receives quests. No combat pressure.
Stacked object environments requiring creative navigation. Vertical space is navigable only through item manipulation. Monster infestations create combat pressure.
Large traversable maps with scattered interactive objects and optional crafting/puzzle content. Real-time movement and encounter-based combat
Areas supporting baking, forging, and creation mechanics. Safe environment encouraging experimentation and item interaction

sources_used: wikipedia: Ultima VII: The Black Gate
